prerequisites: A NASA API key generated for free at https://api.nasa.gov/ by submitting first name, last name, and email (the DEMO_KEY may be used for limited testing).
Network access to the api.nasa.gov host where the NASA Open APIs (including the APOD endpoint) are served.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate by passing your key in the required api_key query-string parameter on every request (for example api_key=DEMO_KEY). The connector stores this value in its api_key secret field. The shared DEMO_KEY works for low-volume testing but is rate limited to 30 requests per IP per hour and 50 per IP per day; a personal key raises the limit to 1,000 requests per hour.

setupSteps.title: Generate a NASA API key
setupSteps.body: Go to https://api.nasa.gov/ and complete the signup form (first name, last name, and email). NASA issues a personal API key immediately. For quick tests you can instead use the literal value DEMO_KEY, subject to its lower rate limits.

setupSteps.title: Provide the api_key credential
setupSteps.body: Set the required api_key field to the key you generated. It is sent as the api_key query-string parameter on each request to api.nasa.gov.

setupSteps.title: Configure the optional APOD parameters
setupSteps.body: Optionally scope the Astronomy Picture of the Day extraction using the connector config fields, all of which map to NASA APOD query parameters: set start_date and end_date (YYYY-MM-DD) to pull a date range; set count (a positive integer up to 100) to fetch that many random images instead of a range; set thumbs (true/false) to include thumbnail URLs for video entries; and set concept_tags (true/false) to include derived concept tags in the response. Note that count cannot be combined with start_date or end_date.

sources.title: nasa/apod-api (Astronomy Picture of the Day API)
